skill.name: writing-assistant
skill.description: Interactive writing assistant for Chinese social media articles. It guides topic selection, outlining, drafting, title creation, formatting, and post-publication diagnosis.
skill.summary: Guide Chinese social media articles from topic idea to publish-ready draft.
skill.author: Ceeon

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many external command indicators, filesystem access, weak crypto, and high entropy. Manual review found Markdown code fences, inline file names, Chinese prose, and a normal install command, with no executable scripts, network calls, prompt injection, obfuscated payload, or secret access.

low_findings.title: Markdown Code Fences Misidentified as External Commands
low_findings.description: False positive. The reported backtick locations are Markdown inline code or fenced examples that describe writing formulas, checklists, and file names. They are not Ruby backtick execution and do not run commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines are Markdown documentation blocks and inline references. No script file, shell invocation, dynamic execution path, or user input execution vector was found.

low_findings.title: Install Example Misidentified as Hidden File Access
low_findings.description: False positive. README.md shows a user-run installation command that copies the skill into the Claude skills directory. This is documentation for installation, not runtime access to hidden files or sensitive data.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The only hidden-directory reference is an explicit install command in a fenced bash example. The skill contains no code that reads or writes that path automatically.

low_findings.title: Chinese Text Misidentified as Obfuscation
low_findings.description: False positive. High entropy warnings correspond to Chinese Markdown content and Unicode diagrams. The reviewed files contain readable writing guidance, not encoded or encrypted payloads.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The content is human-readable Chinese methodology text with ordinary Markdown structure. No compressed, encrypted, base64, or concealed executable content was found.

low_findings.title: Weak Crypto Pattern Not Confirmed
low_findings.description: False positive. SKILL.md line 3 is a natural-language description of the writing assistant. No hash function, cryptographic algorithm, credential handling, or security-sensitive crypto use appears there.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited line is plain metadata text. There is no cryptographic API, digest name, or code path to evaluate.

content.value_statement: Creators often lose time moving from idea to publishable article. This skill provides a structured workflow for topics, outlines, drafts, titles, formatting, and diagnosis.
actual_capabilities: Guides topic selection with audience, context, and information value checks.
Builds outlines using awareness, interest, decision, and action stages.
Reviews drafts against visual, paragraph, terminology, and opening standards.
Creates title options using numbers, contrast, drama, and low entry barriers.
Formats articles for Chinese public account style with headings, spacing, and ASCII diagrams.
Diagnoses weak article performance using stage-specific checklists.
limitations: It provides writing guidance, not automated publishing or analytics integration.
Users must customize positioning, audience personas, and prohibited topics first.
It is designed mainly for Chinese social media and public account articles.
It does not verify factual claims or collect live market data by itself.

use_cases.title: Plan a New Article
use_cases.description: Turn a rough topic into a focused article plan with audience fit, market gap, and information value checks.

use_cases.title: Improve a Draft Before Publishing
use_cases.description: Score a draft with practical checklists for structure, readability, terminology, visuals, title, and formatting.

use_cases.title: Diagnose Poor Performance
use_cases.description: Review weak article results by checking topic fit, outline promise, opening strength, title clarity, and formatting.

prompt_templates.title: Choose a Topic
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the writing-assistant skill to evaluate this article idea: [topic]. Check audience, context, information value, and market gap.

prompt_templates.title: Create an Outline
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the writing-assistant outline method for this topic: [topic]. Include why it matters, who it fits, steps, and next actions.

prompt_templates.title: Review a Draft
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the writing-assistant checklist to review this draft: [paste draft]. Score it and list the highest-impact improvements.

prompt_templates.title: Repair a Low-Performing Article
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the writing-assistant workflow to diagnose why this article underperformed: [article and results]. Check topic, outline, body, title, and formatting.

output_examples.input: I want to write about using AI tools for beginners.
output_examples.output: The skill would narrow the audience, define the learning context, check market saturation, and suggest an article promise for beginners.

output_examples.input: My article had low reads after publishing.
output_examples.output: The skill would diagnose weak topic demand, unclear promise, missing why section, title problems, or formatting friction.

best_practices: Customize about-me.md and user-personas.md before asking for article guidance.
Provide topic goals, target audience, draft text, and any performance data when available.
Use the full sequence from topic to formatting for new articles.
anti_patterns: Do not skip audience definition and ask for a generic article immediately.
Do not treat generated titles as final without checking accuracy and audience fit.
Do not use it as a fact checker for claims that require external verification.

faq.question: What type of writing does this skill support?
faq.answer: It supports Chinese social media articles, especially public account style content from topic planning to formatting.

faq.question: Does it write complete articles?
faq.answer: It can guide drafting, but it works best when users provide positioning, audience details, and source material.

faq.question: Can it review an existing draft?
faq.answer: Yes. It uses checklists for topic fit, outline, body clarity, title strength, and formatting.

faq.question: Does it publish content automatically?
faq.answer: No. It does not connect to publishing platforms or post content for the user.

faq.question: Is it useful for non-Chinese content?
faq.answer: Some planning methods transfer, but examples and formatting rules are designed for Chinese content workflows.

faq.question: What should I prepare before using it?
faq.answer: Prepare your niche, audience personas, forbidden topics, terminology explanations, and any draft or performance data.
